description: In March 2019, following the 'Varsity Blues' college admissions scandal, Sephora faced intense consumer pressure to drop influencer Olivia Jade Giannulli. After a period of initial hesitation, the company severed ties with the influencer, demonstrating the volatility of influencer-led beauty partnerships when personal scandals intersect with ethical public issues.

key_events.description: News breaks of the Varsity Blues college admissions scandal involving Lori Loughlin and daughter Olivia Jade.
Customers flood Sephora's social media accounts demanding they drop the influencer's makeup collection.
Sephora announces the termination of the partnership and removal of the 'Olivia Jade' collection.
General public debate transitions from individual guilt to the ethics of brand-influencer vetting.

calibration_notes: Useful for calibrating the lag time between a crisis event and the necessary corporate response, and the subsequent recovery of brand sentiment.
